description: The late 2021-early 2022 debate regarding the efficacy of the monoclonal antibody sotrovimab against the Omicron variant of SARS-CoV-2. Health agencies struggled to maintain recommendations while data shifted rapidly.

key_events.description: Omicron variant becomes dominant, questioning previous lab efficacy data.
NIH shifts treatment guidelines based on preliminary neutralization assays.
FDA authorizes, then later limits the use of the drug as variants evolve.
Clinical usage drops sharply as newer therapies replace it.

calibration_notes: Focuses on the difficulty of maintaining public consensus in a fast-moving, high-stakes medical environment.
